item.title: Yi (William) Wei
item.description:
Yi (William) Wei

Associate of IQSS
email wwei@harvard.edu

William Wei's research interests include:

  • Comparative study of Eastern and Western cultures
  • Study of moral intelligence, health, and happiness
  • Application of IT and social networks in social science and humanities 

  • People By Last Name

    W - Z

  • Role

    Associates

item.title: Stephen Kraffmiller
item.description:
Stephen Kraffmiller

Software Developer, Dataverse Project
email skraffmiller@hmdc.harvard.edu

Stephen has been a devloper on the Dataverse Project since 2010. He is involved in all aspects of the Dataverse project, from the front end UI development to the back end business logic. For the Dataverse project he was the lead developer for custom dataset metadata fields, persistent identifiers, guestbook and download tracking.  He also implemented dataset templates and external query tools. He has given three presentations at JavaOne conferences over the years including "jQuery Integration with JSF 2 Libraries" with Mike Heppler and "Custom Bean Validation" with Gustavo Durand.  Currently he is spearheading the effort to make Dataverse data available via the Google Data Commons. In his spare time he enjoys running, reading, cooking and following the career of a talented playwright who happens to be his daughter.

item.title: Robert Treacy
item.description:
Robert Treacy

Principal Software Architect and Data Engineer, Dataverse Project
email rtreacy@hmdc.harvard.edu

Bob Treacy is a Principal Software Architect and Data Engineer. He came to IQSS in 2006 at the beginning stages of the Dataverse Project and participated in the initial architecture, design and development of the system. He worked on a project for survey research, the Consilience project for text analysis, differential privacy, and maintained the Zelig project and various other R packages when they were hosted on CRAN. He has been working with Java since 1995, when he was at Fusion Systems Group, a consulting firm which worked closely with Sun Microsystems. He has over 35 years in software development for manufacturing, financial systems, education course management, news delivery and various web companies. In addition to Java, he has developed in Scala, C/C++, Python, and R. He has worked with various relational databases and NoSQL databases. His first distributed systems project in the early 90s involved integrating stove-piped legacy terminal-based manufacturing systems using modern UIs and in recent years has worked with Hadoop and Spark for machine learning and AI. He is currently involved with fine-tuning local Large Language Models using FAS RC on Dataverse data. He has worked with the AWS and Azure cloud platforms.

item.title: Julian Gautier
item.description:
Julian Gautier

Product Research Specialist
email juliangautier@g.harvard.edu

Julian Gautier joined IQSS in September 2016 as a product research specialist for the Data Science and Product Research team, responsible for providing and extending Dataverse metadata support, and helping collect software requirements from product leaders and its community of users.

item.title: Jeremy Yuenger
item.description:
Jeremy Yuenger

IT Client Support Services II, Harvard-MIT Data Center
email help@iq.harvard.edu

Jeremy joined IQSS in 2013, and most recently has been serving in an application specialist role supporting the Dataverse Project and the FASRC cluster. In his spare time, he enjoys building mechanical keyboards, learning circuitry and PCB design, photography, and curating a vast collection of bad movies.

item.title: Ellen Kraffmiller
item.description:
Ellen Kraffmiller

Principal Software Architect and Data Engineer
email ekraffmiller@hmdc.harvard.edu

Ellen Kraffmiller is a software engineer who has been at IQSS for over ten years.  Previously, she worked in software consulting, financial services and biotech industries.  She has contributed to the Dataverse and Consilience projects. She has expertise in distributed computing, machine learning and natural language processing.

item.title: Dan O'Brien
item.description:
Dan O'Brien

Associate of IQSS
email d.obrien@northeastern.edu

Dan O’Brien's work focuses on the ways that researchers, policymakers, and practitioners can work together to leverage modern digital data (i.e., “Big Data”) to better understand and serve cities. His own work focuses on the behavioral and social dynamics of urban neighborhoods, particularly those that directly impact a place’s future upward (or downward) trajectory.

item.title: Courtney Hayes
item.description:
Courtney Hayes

Feature Writer
email courtneyhayes@mac.com
laptop_windows Website

Courtney Hayes is an award-winning writer and documentary filmmaker. Courtney began her career at Frontline, PBS' premier public affairs documentary series. She has since written and produced documentary films for The History Channel, Discovery, and Curiosity Stream, as well as high-performing digital stories for leading brands. Courtney is currently writing interview-based feature articles for IQSS.

item.title: Chetan Krishna
item.description:
Chetan Krishna

Research Associate, Laboratory for Innovation Science
email ckrishna@hbs.edu

Chetan Krishna is a research associate focusing on the processes of science and technology commercialization and the adoption of AI/ML/NLP in businesses. His interests lie broadly in innovation and energy/sustainability. Chetan received his master’s degree from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology’s Technology Policy Program and received a bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi.

item.title: Philip Durbin
item.description:
Philip Durbin

Software Developer, Dataverse Project
email philip_durbin@harvard.edu
laptop_windows greptilian.com
  • Twitter
  • Linkedin

With a degree in Psychology from the University of Dayton, Phil is happy to be working at an institute dedicated to social science and proud to be part of the team that rewrote Dataverse to be more inclusive of all science. It's hard to stop him from jumping up and giving an impromptu talk on Dataverse.
